queries: multi-project workspace management cross-project data pipeline design best practices 2026
key_findings: dbt Mesh is the leading multi-project data pipeline framework in 2026: cross-project ref() references, model access levels (public/protected/private), cycle detection, unified lineage. dbt-loom open-source plugin for dbt-core users.

Project splitting strategies: Vertical (layer-based) for shared foundations + Horizontal (domain-based) aligned with team ownership. Combined strategy recommended. Rule: align project boundaries with team boundaries.

Repository strategy: Monorepo for smaller teams/simpler coordination; Multi-repo for independent deploy cadences/security isolation. Trigger point for split: ~5,000+ models in single project or weekly cross-project changes >20% of work.
Workspace organization: one workspace per developer, three-environment structure (dev/staging/prod), template-driven provisioning covering 80-90% of configuration.
Cross-workspace control planes (2026 trend): Dagster WorkspaceComponent auto-discovers assets across multiple workspaces → single observable asset graph with zero code changes.

Key pitfalls: lost source freshness checks (use ephemeral staging models + recency tests), metadata leakage (use access levels), bidirectional dependency cycles (stepwise project setup), performance at scale.

x-tool applicability: Current 6 core projects are functionally independent (no data pipeline dependencies) — dbt Mesh patterns overkill. But template-driven provisioning (one developer workspace pattern) and three-environment structure (dev→staging→prod per project) are applicable reference architecture.
Asana Work Graph 2026: cross-project dependency visualization without code coupling — conceptual model for multi-project workspaces where projects share infrastructure but operate independently.
research.note: 1 query, 7+ independent sources (dbt docs, Dagster, Asana, Integrate.io, Ascend.io) converging on same 2026 consensus. Multi-project workspace management is a NEW domain — first coverage in 13 L3 cycles. Prior domains: autonomous agents v1-v7, web scraping v1-v7, quant backtesting v1-v2, social media automation, AI content generation, factor mining automation. This domain is less directly applicable to code-heavy x-tool workspace than prior research but provides architectural reference for workspace organization patterns.
